summary.intent: Fix build errors in the moltis Rust workspace caused by a rand 0.10 upgrade breaking existing imports across multiple crates.
summary.outcome: All rand 0.10 API breakage was resolved across 12 files by replacing `rand::RngCore` with `rand::Rng` (for `fill_bytes`) and adding `rand::RngExt` (for `random()` and `random_range()`). The workspace compiled cleanly and changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The moltis workspace uses rand and had widespread use of pre-0.10 APIs: `rand::RngCore`, `fill_bytes`, and `random()`/`random_range()` spread across oauth, vault, webhooks, browser, auth, cli, and gateway crates.
Not all affected crates were compiled in the first build pass ‚Äî additional files (auth, cli, gateway) still had `rand::RngCore` imports discovered via grep after the initial fixes.

code.finding: Used `rand::RngCore` import and `fill_bytes` ‚Äî needs `rand::Rng` in rand 0.10
Used `rand::RngCore::fill_bytes` qualified path ‚Äî needs trait in scope via `rand::Rng`

workflow: In rand 0.10: `RngCore` moved to `rand_core` crate; `fill_bytes` is now accessed via `rand::Rng` trait; `random()` and `random_range()` moved to `rand::RngExt` trait.
Grepping for `rand::RngCore` after fixing compiler-reported errors is essential ‚Äî Cargo's parallel build stops early and doesn't surface all affected crates in one pass.
Running a full workspace check (`cargo check --workspace`) after targeted fixes catches remaining issues before committing.

friction: Cargo's parallel build stops on first crate failure, requiring multiple fix-compile cycles to discover all affected files.
The rand 0.10 API split (`Rng` vs `RngExt`) is non-obvious ‚Äî `fill_bytes` and `random()` moved to different traits despite both being on `ThreadRng`.
open_items: Unused `rand::Rng` import warnings in webhooks and browser were present but only warned ‚Äî worth cleaning up to keep the codebase warning-free.
Consider auditing all crates for rand version pinning to prevent similar breakage on future upgrades.
